title: Cell Lysis Protocol Without Detergents
author: Queenie Chan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.k79czr6

steps.title: Fresh or previously frozen, store on ice
steps.instruction: Fresh or previously frozen, store on ice. Original protocol uses 5x10 7 cells per sample.

steps.title: 1:1 water-TFE acts as a hypotonic...
steps.instruction: 1:1 water-TFE acts as a hypotonic aqueous buffer to lyse cells, eliminating the need for detergent. TFE helps protein solubility and denaturation; it readily evaporates, so removing it is easy. undetermined, Trifluoroethanol TFE is located in NCE 435 - flammable cabinet TFE evaporates fast, so work quickly. This step should be done in the fume hood.

steps.title: Original protcol calls for 2 mins...
steps.instruction: Original protcol calls for 2 mins at 20% duty cycle, output control 3 using a ultrasonic probe. The time required to do this for large number of samples is not realistic, and therefore not used here. 00:02:00, in water bath benchtop sonicator While this step is happening, turn on the heat block and set it at 95oC

steps.title: Add TCEP (tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine to a final...
steps.instruction: Add TCEP (tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine to a final concentration of 10mM TCEP is located at NCE 436 -20C freezer door

steps.title: Add 2-chloroacetamide to a final concentration...
steps.instruction: Add 2-chloroacetamide to a final concentration of 40mM 2-chloroacetamide is located in NCE 438 - chemical shelves 00:10:00, 95oC in a heat block

steps.title: Spindown debris at top speed on...
steps.instruction: Spindown debris at top speed on a benchtop centrifuge for 10 minutes, then keep the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 1ug LysC to every 50ug...
steps.instruction: Add 1ug LysC to every 50ug protein 02:00:00 Undetermined, incubator (not heat block)

steps.title: Add 1ug trypsin to every 50 ug protein
steps.instruction: Add 1ug trypsin to every 50 ug protein. Allow reaction to occur overnight. 16:00:00, minimum reaction time Undetermined, incubator (not heat block)

steps.title: Use the Coomassie reagent to measure protein concentration
steps.instruction: Use the Coomassie reagent to measure protein concentration. Aliquot the desired amount to proceed with the remainder of this experiment. Freezing unused protein is not recommended since there is a high chance of precipitation upon thawing.
